name: MIT Launch Summer

cost.notes: Need-based scholarships available; MIT covers full demonstrated need

essays.prompt: Describe a problem you want to solve and why it matters to you.
tips: MIT Launch values genuine problem-solving drive, not business jargon
Show personal connection to the problem

outcomes.college_impact: Positive signal at MIT and other top engineering/business schools. MIT Launch alumni frequently start real companies.
outcomes.typical_results: Many alumni start actual businesses during or after the program. Strong placement at MIT, Stanford, Wharton, and other top entrepreneurial programs.

what_they_look_for: Genuine entrepreneurial drive and problem-solving orientation
Curiosity about technology and its applications
Initiative — MIT Launch wants students who will take action, not just attend

blurbs.short: A 4-week MIT summer entrepreneurship program where students form teams, develop a real startup, and pitch to investors — for real.
blurbs.long: MIT Launch is MIT's official summer entrepreneurship program and it's unlike most other summer programs: students don't just learn about entrepreneurship, they do it. Over 4 weeks, teams of students identify a real problem, develop a product or service to address it, create a business model, and pitch to actual investors at a final Demo Day. The program draws on MIT's legendary entrepreneurial ecosystem. What's distinctive about MIT Launch is that it's a genuine startup accelerator for high school students — teams have launched real companies during the program, some of which have gone on to raise funding and acquire users. The program also covers topics like financial modeling, marketing, and legal structure. For students interested in technology entrepreneurship, MIT Launch is unmatched.

curriculum: Ideation, customer discovery, business model development, prototyping, investor pitch preparation, mentorship from MIT alumni entrepreneurs, Demo Day.
